Coagulation

Test01-OCT-2017Reference RangeUnit

aPTT in Blood by Coagulation assay35.1 H22.0 - 32.0s

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows marked hyperbilirubinemia, elevated alkaline phosphatase, and prolonged aPTT, suggesting possible cholestasis or liver dysfunction. Creatinine is significantly elevated, indicating impaired renal function. Severe metabolic acidosis is present (pH 6.9, low CO₂), which is life-threatening. Coagulation abnormalities (low PT/INR, high aPTT) and mild hyperlipidemia are also noted. Immediate clinical evaluation is urgently recommended.
